title: Distribution connection manager — TCP connections and connection table
purpose: Distribution connections are long-lived TCP connections between nodes. The connection manager owns the connection table (mapping node names to active connections), handles outbound connection setup (resolve → connect → handshake), accepts inbound connections, and detects connection failures. Each connection is a bidirectional byte stream used for control messages and term data.
task: Implement the ConnectionManager with a connection table, outbound connect path, inbound accept loop, and connection lifecycle management.

requirements.title: Connection table and ConnectionManager
requirements.spec: ConnectionManager owns a map of active distribution connections indexed by node name.
acceptance: ConnectionManager struct with connections: DashMap<Atom, Arc<DistConnection>>
DistConnection wraps a TCP stream (tokio::net::TcpStream or equivalent) with read/write halves
get_connection(node: Atom) -> Option<Arc<DistConnection>> looks up active connection
ConnectionManager::start(listen_addr, resolver) spawns the accept loop
Test: create manager, verify no connections initially

requirements.title: Outbound connection establishment
requirements.spec: Connect to a remote node by resolving its name and establishing a TCP connection.
acceptance: connect(node_name: &str) -> Result<Arc<DistConnection>, ConnectError> resolves name via NodeResolver, opens TCP connection
Connection is added to the connection table on success
Test: connect to a local listener, verify connection established

requirements.title: Inbound connection accept loop
requirements.spec: Listen for incoming distribution connections and add them to the connection table after identification.
acceptance: Accept loop runs on a dedicated async task
Incoming connections are held pending until the handshake identifies the remote node (B-115)
Accepted connections are added to the connection table with the remote node name as key
Test: spawn two ConnectionManagers, connect one to the other, verify bidirectional connection

requirements.title: Connection failure detection and cleanup
requirements.spec: Detect TCP connection failures and remove dead connections from the table.
acceptance: Connection read/write errors trigger connection removal from the table
A connection_down callback notifies the distribution layer (for link/monitor cleanup in B-122/B-121)
Reconnection is NOT automatic (net_kernel handles that in B-123)
Test: establish connection, drop remote end, verify connection removed from table

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement the handshake protocol — that's B-115
SHALL NOT implement message encoding/decoding — that's B-117
SHALL NOT implement automatic reconnection — that's B-123's net_kernel responsibility
SHALL use async I/O (tokio) for connection management
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
